prompt: The line graph shows the number of visitors to three museums in London between 2010 and 2020.

visual.alt: Line graph comparing visitor numbers for the British Museum, Science Museum and Natural History Museum from 2010 to 2020
visual.instruction: Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

method.title: 折线图通用四段法：趋势分组 + 对比

paragraphs.label: 第1段 · 改写题目
paragraphs.text: 换词或换句型，交代比较对象、数据指标和时间范围，不照抄题干。

paragraphs.label: 第2段 · Overview
paragraphs.text: 用 Overall 概括最显著的两个特点；优先观察总体趋势、最高或最低项及明显拐点，不写具体数字。

paragraphs.label: 第3段 · Detail 1
paragraphs.text: 把对象按相近趋势或水平分成两组，本段写第一组；选择起点、终点、峰值、低点或拐点等关键数据，并尽量作比较。

paragraphs.label: 第4段 · Detail 2
paragraphs.text: 写另一组对象，继续选择关键数据，进行组内或跨组比较，不逐年、逐点罗列全部数字。

method.transfer: 过去收集的数据用一般过去时。只客观描述数据，不解释原因。四段是稳定训练框架，但不必硬凑 11 句或每个 Detail 4 句；信息写全即可，全文仍须达到 150 词。

chain: 改写题目 → Overview先写三馆都上升+British Museum始终最高 → 两组细节数据
script: The line graph illustrates the number of visitors to three museums in London between 2010 and 2020. Overall, it is clear that all three museums saw an increase, with the British Museum remaining the most popular throughout. In 2010, the British Museum received around 5 million visitors, and this figure rose steadily to about 6.5 million in 2020. The Science Museum, meanwhile, started at just 2 million and climbed gradually to 3.5 million, while the Natural History Museum fluctuated around 3 million before ending at roughly 4 million.
keySentences: Overall, it is clear that all three museums saw an increase.
The British Museum remained the most popular throughout.
This figure rose steadily from X to Y.
transfer: 不看模板，写出另一张折线图的改写句和两句 Overview。
note: 已背模板复习，不是完整答案；真实考试必须扩展到 150 词以上。

prompt: The bar chart compares household internet access in four countries in 2010 and 2020.

visual.alt: Grouped bar chart comparing household internet access in the UK, Germany, France and Italy in 2010 and 2020
visual.instruction: Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

transferVisual.label: 柱状图 → 饼图迁移题
transferVisual.prompt: The pie charts show how average household expenditure in a city was distributed among five categories in 2000 and 2020.
transferVisual.instruction: Write a paraphrase, two Overview sentences and two data comparisons.
transferVisual.alt: Two pie charts comparing average household expenditure on food, housing, transport, leisure and other items in 2000 and 2020

method.title: 柱状图四段法（同框架覆盖饼图）

paragraphs.label: 第1段 · 改写题目
paragraphs.text: 换词或换句型，交代比较对象、指标、地点或类别，以及题目给出的时间。

paragraphs.label: 第2段 · Overview
paragraphs.text: 用 Overall 概括最显著的两个特点，如最高最低、主导类别、共同模式或异常项；只有带时间变化时才分析趋势和拐点。

paragraphs.label: 第3段 · Detail 1
paragraphs.text: 按高低、相似程度、类别或时间把对象分成两组，本段写第一组；选择关键数据，并比较差异或相似点。

paragraphs.label: 第4段 · Detail 2
paragraphs.text: 写另一组对象，继续围绕关键数据作比较，不按每根柱子机械报数。

method.transfer: 饼图不用新背一套框架。只补 4 句：The pie charts compare the proportions of X in Y. / Overall, X accounted for the largest share, whereas Y made up the smallest. / X represented A%, compared with B% for Y. / Together, X and Y accounted for C% of the total. 单年份只比占比；有不同年份才写上升或下降。

chain: 改写题目 → Overview写四国都上升+英国两年最高 → 按高低两组比较数据
script: The bar chart compares the percentage of households with internet access in four countries — the UK, Germany, France and Italy — in 2010 and 2020. Overall, it is clear that internet access increased in all four countries over the decade, with the UK having the highest level in both years. In 2010, around 70% of UK households had internet access, and this rose to roughly 95% by 2020. Germany followed a similar pattern, climbing from about 65% to 90%. France and Italy started lower, at approximately 55% and 50% respectively, but both saw significant growth, reaching around 80% and 75% by the end of the period.
keySentences: Overall, internet access increased in all four countries.
The UK had the highest level in both years.
Germany followed a similar pattern, climbing from X to Y.
transfer: 用已背的柱状图框架，给一张饼图写改写句、两句 Overview，并按占比高低分成两组。
note: 已背柱状模板的正文和音频不变；饼图只做语言迁移，不新背整篇范文。真实考试仍须写到 150 词以上。

prompt: Some people believe that new technology makes life more complicated, so people should use less of it. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

method.title: Opinion 通用四段法：明确立场 + 两个分论点

paragraphs.label: 第1段 · 开头
paragraphs.text: 改写题目并明确立场：完全同意、完全不同意或部分同意；不要把题目改写成另一个问题。

paragraphs.label: 第2段 · 中间段 1
paragraphs.text: 用主题句提出第一分论点，再用理由、进一步解释和例子的任意组合展开。

paragraphs.label: 第3段 · 中间段 2
paragraphs.text: 提出第二分论点并继续支持开头立场；部分同意时可分析另一面，但篇幅要体现最终倾向。

paragraphs.label: 第4段 · 结尾
paragraphs.text: 换一种说法重申开头立场，不加入新的分论点。

method.transfer: 开头、主体段和结尾的立场必须一致。每个理由都要直接回答新题，不能套用旧范文的具体内容；通用模板只规定段落功能，不规定固定句子。

chain: 立场:总体不同意 → 承认分心和依赖 → 科技节省时间并扩大服务可及性 → 选择性使用,而非全面拒绝
script: It is often argued that modern technology has made daily life unnecessarily complex and that people should reduce their use of it. Although digital tools can create distraction and dependence, I largely disagree with this view because, when used sensibly, they save time and improve access to essential services.

Admittedly, technology can make life harder when people allow it to control their attention. Constant messages interrupt work, while frequent software updates and complicated settings can frustrate older users. In addition, relying on a phone for every small task may weaken basic skills, such as remembering directions or managing time independently. These problems explain why setting limits, especially during study and rest, is reasonable.

However, reducing technology in general would ignore its practical value. Online banking, navigation and appointment systems allow people to complete routine tasks quickly, which is particularly helpful for workers with limited free time. Technology also gives students access to courses and information that may not be available locally. In the workplace, shared documents and video meetings help teams cooperate without travelling, reducing travel costs and wasted time.

In my view, the better solution is selective use rather than broad rejection. People can turn off unnecessary notifications, choose simple applications and keep some screen-free periods while still using tools that clearly improve their lives. Schools and employers should also teach basic digital skills so that people can use technology confidently rather than find it stressful. Overall, the disadvantages come mainly from poor habits and design, not from technology itself, and these issues can be managed without giving up its benefits.
keySentences: Although digital tools can create problems, I largely disagree with this view.
These problems explain why setting reasonable limits is necessary.
However, rejecting this development would ignore its practical value.
The better solution is selective use rather than broad rejection.
transfer: Some people believe that schools should replace printed textbooks with digital devices. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
note: 目标是记住立场、两条论证链和核心句，再换题重建；不要在考场逐字套全文。

prompt: The diagram shows how food waste from a school cafeteria is converted into compost for the school gardens.

method.title: 流程图通用四段骨架：每段有固定衔接槽位

paragraphs.label: 第1段 · 改写题目
paragraphs.text: 改写题干，说明图中展示什么过程，或某种原料、产品如何被处理、生产或回收。

paragraphs.label: 第2段 · Overview
paragraphs.text: 用 Overall 概括总阶段数、起点和终点；优先套 consists of ... stages, beginning with ... and ending with ...。

paragraphs.label: 第3段 · Detail 1
paragraphs.text: 按自然阶段分成两组；用 At the first stage 起手，再用 is then ... 和 after which ... 串起第一组动作。

paragraphs.label: 第4段 · Detail 2
paragraphs.text: 用 In the next stage 转入第二组；用 During ... 写时间跨度，When ... is complete 写阶段完成，最后用 Finally 收尾。

method.transfer: 连接词按功能背，不背长清单：①总览边界 beginning with / ending with；②阶段起手 At the first stage / In the next stage；③句内串联 is then ... / after which ...；④时间与完成 During ... / When ... is complete / Finally。全篇以一般现在时和被动语态为主；不要每句都在句首塞 First / Then，也不要用 In conclusion。

chain: 固定骨架：改写 → Overall（阶段数 + beginning with / ending with）→ At the first stage / after which → In the next stage / During / When / Finally｜本题只填：收集去塑料 → 切碎混干叶 → 通气翻动 → 筛分用于花园

visual.alt: Black-and-white process diagram showing six stages from collecting cafeteria food waste to using screened compost in school gardens
visual.instruction: Summarise the process by selecting and reporting its main stages.

script: The diagram illustrates how food waste from a school cafeteria is converted into compost for use in the school gardens.

Overall, the process consists of six main stages, beginning with the collection of food waste and ending with the use of screened compost in the gardens. The composting stage itself takes about eight weeks.

At the first stage, food waste is collected in a bin in the cafeteria. Any plastic is then removed from the waste, after which the remaining material is chopped into small pieces and mixed with an equal amount of dry leaves.

In the next stage, this mixture is placed in a covered compost bin with holes that allow air to enter. During the following eight weeks, the material is turned once a week. When the composting period is complete, the compost is passed through a screen so that larger pieces are removed. Finally, the finished product is taken to the school gardens, where it is spread around plants.
keySentences: The diagram illustrates how X is processed into Y.
Overall, the process consists of X main stages, beginning with A and ending with B.
At the first stage, X is collected and prepared.
X is then processed, after which it is mixed with Y.
During the following X weeks, the material is treated regularly. When this stage is complete, it is processed again.
Finally, the finished product is taken to Y, where it is used for Z.
transfer: The diagram shows how used paper is collected and processed into new notebooks. 只写流程链、Overview和一个细节段。
note: 重点背四段骨架和 6 个衔接句块，把 food waste / compost 等具体名词当作 X / Y 替换槽位。162 词范文可按段跟读，但不必死背题目专有名词；连接结构按功能使用，不追求生僻同义词。

cn: 大学应就业导向还是广泛教育
prompt: Some people think that the main purpose of university education is to prepare students for employment. Others believe that universities should provide a broad education. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

method.title: Discussion 通用四段法：讨论双方 + 给出立场

paragraphs.label: 第1段 · 开头
paragraphs.text: 改写题目中的两种观点，并明确自己更支持一方还是持折中立场。

paragraphs.label: 第2段 · 观点 A
paragraphs.text: 先写主题句，再用理由、进一步解释和例子说明为什么有人持第一种观点。

paragraphs.label: 第3段 · 观点 B
paragraphs.text: 同样完整展开第二种观点，并让论证权重与自己的最终立场保持一致。

paragraphs.label: 第4段 · 结尾
paragraphs.text: 简洁总结讨论并重申个人立场，不加入新理由。

method.transfer: 题目要求 discuss both views 时，两方都必须得到实质分析，不能只列出观点或只写自己同意的一方。个人立场可以偏向一方或折中，但开头、论证和结尾必须一致。

chain: 两方都有价值+主张结合 → 就业技能回应成本和职场需要 → 广泛教育培养可迁移能力 → 基础知识+实习/职业任务
script: People disagree about whether the main purpose of university is to prepare students for a specific career or to offer a wider education. Both aims are valuable, but I believe universities should combine them, with practical preparation built on a broad academic foundation.

Those who favour job-focused courses point to the high cost of higher education. Students often invest several years and a substantial amount of money in a degree, so they reasonably expect useful employment skills in return. Work placements, laboratory training and realistic group projects can make graduates more confident and reduce the amount of training employers must provide. This is especially important in professional fields such as engineering, nursing and teaching, where mistakes can have serious consequences.

On the other hand, a university is not simply a training centre for one current job. Industries change, and a narrow technical skill may become outdated within a few years. Subjects such as history, science and philosophy teach students to examine evidence, explain ideas and consider different viewpoints. These abilities can be transferred to many careers. A broader education also helps graduates make informed decisions as citizens, rather than judging every subject only by its immediate financial value.

In my opinion, the two purposes should support each other. Degree programmes can include a strong core of subject knowledge, together with optional internships and career-related assignments. This arrangement would help students enter the labour market while retaining the ability to learn and adapt later. Therefore, universities should not choose between employment and education; they should prepare students for their first job and for the changes that will follow it.
keySentences: Both aims are valuable, but I believe they should be combined.
Supporters of this view point to the practical benefits.
On the other hand, a university is not simply a training centre for one current job.
The two purposes should support each other rather than compete.
transfer: Some people think working from home benefits employees, while others believe it damages teamwork. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
note: 两方都要解释和支持；个人立场从引言到结论保持一致。

prompt: The maps show the Riverside district in 2000 and at present.

method.title: 地图题通用四段法：最大变化 + 分区对照

paragraphs.label: 第1段 · 改写题目
paragraphs.text: 改写题干，说明比较地点和两个时间。句型：The maps compare the layout of X in two different periods.

paragraphs.label: 第2段 · Overview
paragraphs.text: 写最大整体转变，再补主要替换和保留项。句型：Overall, the area has changed from X into Y. / X has been replaced by Y, while Z remains unchanged.

paragraphs.label: 第3段 · Detail 1
paragraphs.text: 先写旧图，按区域交代设施、准确位置和缺失项；只选能帮助对照新图的信息。

paragraphs.label: 第4段 · Detail 2
paragraphs.text: 再写新图，用 former site / is occupied by / has been built / has been widened 串起替换、新增、扩建与保留。

method.transfer: 时态跟地图时间一致：过去图用过去时，现在图用现在时，规划图用将来表达。只客观描述空间变化，不评价规划好坏；Detail 不必各写 4 句，分组也不必平均。

chain: 地点+两个时间 → 工业区变住宅休闲区+保留项 → 2000按北岸/南岸写 → 现在按原位置写替换/新增/扩建

visual.alt: Two maps comparing the Riverside district in 2000 and at present
visual.instruction: Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
before: 西侧主路旁:一排仓储建筑
after: 原仓储建筑位置:超市和停车场
南岸公园拓宽并新增咖啡馆和自行车道
新增人行桥连接北侧住宅区和南岸公园

script: The maps compare the layout of the Riverside district in two different periods: 2000 and the present.

Overall, the area has changed from an industrial district into a residential and leisure neighbourhood. The industrial buildings have been replaced by homes and facilities, while the river and main road remain unchanged.

In 2000, three factories stood north of the river. A row of storage buildings was located beside the main road in the west, while a small group of houses occupied the northeast corner. South of the river, there was only a narrow park, and no bridge connected the two banks.

Today, the former factory sites are occupied by apartment blocks, while the storage area has become a supermarket and a car park. The original houses remain, and a new school has been built immediately to their west. The park has been widened and now contains a cafe and a cycle path. Finally, a footbridge connects the residential area with the park, whereas the main road follows the same route as before.
keySentences: The maps compare the layout of X in two different periods.
Overall, the area has changed from X into Y.
X has been replaced by Y, while Z remains unchanged.
The former X sites are occupied by Y.
A new X has been built immediately to the west of Y.
X has been widened and now contains Y, whereas Z follows the same route as before.
transfer: The maps show a public park in 1990 and after its recent redevelopment. 只写变化分组、Overview和一个细节段。
note: 重点背最大变化、保留项和位置表达；不要逐个设施机械点名。

prompt: Traffic congestion and air pollution have become serious problems in many cities. What problems do they cause, and what measures can governments and individuals take to solve them?

method.title: Two-Part 通用四段法：逐问作答

paragraphs.label: 第1段 · 开头
paragraphs.text: 改写题目背景，并用一句话同时回应或预告两个问题的答案方向。

paragraphs.label: 第2段 · 回答第 1 问
paragraphs.text: 用主题句直接回答第一问，再以理由、进一步解释和例子的任意组合展开。

paragraphs.label: 第3段 · 回答第 2 问
paragraphs.text: 用主题句直接回答第二问，展开方式与上一段相同，不能把第二问压缩到结尾。

paragraphs.label: 第4段 · 结尾
paragraphs.text: 换一种说法概括两个答案，不引入新的问题、原因或措施。

method.transfer: Problem-Solution 只是 Two-Part 的一种。换题后必须按实际两问重排内容，例如原因与影响、原因与是否积极等；两问都要分别且充分作答，不能机械套问题加措施。

chain: 概括问题+预告方案 → 堵车浪费时间/降低效率 + 尾气危害健康 → 政府改善公交/公交专线/衔接/骑行道 + 拥堵费/排放标准 → 个人公交/步行/骑行/拼车/错峰 → 政策与个人选择结合
script: Traffic congestion and air pollution are increasingly common in large cities. They waste time, damage people’s health and reduce the quality of urban life. These problems can be tackled through better public transport and sensible restrictions on car use, while individuals can choose cleaner ways to travel.

One major problem caused by congestion is the loss of time. Commuters may spend hours stuck in traffic and arrive at work tired and stressed, while buses are delayed and deliveries take longer. This can lower productivity and leave people with less time for their families or leisure activities. Traffic is also a major source of air pollution in cities. Exhaust fumes from slow-moving and idling vehicles can worsen respiratory problems and make streets less pleasant for walking or exercise. Children, older people and those with existing health conditions may be particularly affected.

Governments should first make public transport reliable, affordable and convenient. For example, they can introduce dedicated bus lanes, improve connections between bus and rail services, and build safe cycling paths. They can also introduce congestion charges in busy city centres and enforce stricter vehicle-emission standards. However, suitable alternatives to driving should be available before such restrictions are imposed. Individuals can contribute by using public transport, walking or cycling for short journeys, and sharing cars when necessary. They can also avoid driving during peak hours where possible.

In conclusion, traffic congestion wastes time and reduces productivity, while polluted air threatens public health. Governments have the main responsibility for improving transport systems and regulating vehicles, but individual choices are also important. A combination of effective public policies and less dependence on private cars is likely to bring lasting improvement.
keySentences: One major problem caused by X is Y.
This can lower X and leave people with less time for Y.
Governments should first make X reliable, affordable and convenient.
However, suitable alternatives should be available before such restrictions are imposed.
Individuals can contribute by X, Y or Z.
A combination of effective public policies and individual choices is likely to bring lasting improvement.
transfer: Many cities are producing increasing amounts of household waste. What problems does this cause, and what measures can reduce it?
note: 这是双问题题的一种结构：主体一答问题，主体二答措施；其他 Two-Part 仍必须按实际两问更换全部论证内容。
